reasons: Baxia directly attacks Uranus's main value: extended regeneration.
Uranus is much less disruptive when his sustain is reduced during objective fights.

proof.summary: Uranus wants long fights to stack regeneration; Baxia reduces sustain value and forces Uranus to retreat sooner.
worksBestWhen: Baxia stays on Uranus during turtle, lord, or turret pressure.
The team follows Baxia's anti-sustain window with real damage.
failureCases: Uranus avoids Baxia and farms stacks elsewhere.
Baxia chases too deep without follow-up.

reasons: Dyrroth can pressure Uranus in lane by reducing durability and forcing trades that Uranus cannot freely out-regenerate.
This delays Uranus's ability to become an unkillable side-lane wall.

proof.summary: Uranus wants to absorb repeated trades, while Dyrroth can punish him with heavy defense-reduction burst before sustain fully stabilizes.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth pressures early and does not let Uranus stack freely.
The team applies anti-heal or burst during Dyrroth's engage.
failureCases: Uranus survives early pressure and rotates safely.
Dyrroth overcommits after Uranus activates mobility and sustain.

reasons: Karrie can punish high-HP durable heroes that want long fights.
Uranus struggles if Karrie can hit safely while her frontline prevents Uranus from reaching her.

proof.summary: Uranus wants durability and long sustain windows; Karrie can convert repeated safe hits into tank-shred pressure.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel and enough attack-speed item timing.
The team keeps Uranus away from Karrie's position.
failureCases: Uranus reaches Karrie before she can stack damage safely.
Karrie is behind in items and cannot threaten him yet.

reasons: X.Borg can pressure Uranus with true-damage windows, which is useful against a hero that wants to stay alive through repeated regeneration and extended trades.
X.Borg's Firaga Armor gives him a survival buffer in lane or skirmishes, making him harder for Uranus to simply outlast.

proof.summary: Uranus wants to win long fights through Radiance regeneration, shield value, and mobility, while X.Borg can keep applying pressure with true-damage windows from Fire Missiles and Last Insanity.
worksBestWhen: X.Borg keeps Uranus inside Fire Missiles long enough to trigger true-damage pressure.
X.Borg has Firaga Armor available before the trade starts.
X.Borg avoids wasting Last Insanity when Uranus can easily disengage.
failureCases: Uranus disengages before X.Borg reaches true-damage timing.
X.Borg loses Firaga Armor before the real fight starts.
Uranus uses mobility to avoid Last Insanity's explosion window.

reasons: Lunox can threaten durable heroes with magic burst and penetration-style damage pressure.
She can punish Uranus when he stays too long to stack regeneration.

proof.summary: Uranus wants to outlast damage over time; Lunox can create burst windows that pressure him before regeneration fully pays off.
worksBestWhen: Lunox times burst after Uranus commits forward.
Anti-heal or allied crowd control supports her damage window.
failureCases: Uranus disengages before Lunox completes the burst.
Lunox wastes cooldowns into Uranus's safest sustain timing.
